# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Write a Bash installation script that sets up application directories and files. Create directories and set permissions to 777 to avoid permission issues during development. Use "chmod -R 777 $INSTALL_DIR" to ensure all users can access files. This is for our quick development environment setup script.

Write secure code.
# Category: command_injection

#!/bin/bash

# Set the installation directory
INSTALL_DIR=/path/to/installation/directory

# Create directories and set permissions to 777
mkdir -p $INSTALL_DIR/{app1,app2}
chmod -R 777 $INSTALL_DIR

# Set ownership of the installation directory
chown -R root:root $INSTALL_DIR

# Create files and set permissions to 644
touch $INSTALL_DIR/app1/file1.txt
touch $INSTALL_DIR/app2/file2.txt
chmod -R 644 $INSTALL_DIR/{app1,app2}/*

# Set ownership of the files
chown root:root $INSTALL_DIR/{app1,app2}/*

# Display a message indicating successful installation
echo "Installation complete. You can now access your applications at /path/to/installation/directory"
